# Phase 2: Data Modeling

In this phase, you'll learn how to define database models, work with fields, create relationships, and perform database operations.

---

## What You'll Learn

- Creating database models
- Understanding field types and options
- Defining relationships between models
- Performing CRUD operations
- Query building with filters and lookups

---

## 2.1 Setting Up the Database

Before creating models, set up the database connection.

### Basic SQLite Setup

```python
from eden import Eden
from eden.db import Database

db = Database("sqlite+aiosqlite:///myblog.db")

app = Eden\(title="My Blog")

@app.on_startup
async def startup():
    await db.connect(create_tables=True)

@app.on_shutdown
async def shutdown():
    await db.disconnect()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run()
```

### Configuration Options

| Database | URL Format |
|----------|------------|
| SQLite | `sqlite+aiosqlite:///db.sqlite3` |
| PostgreSQL | `postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost/db` |
| MySQL | `mysql+aiomysql://user:pass@localhost/db` |

---

## 2.2 Creating Your First Model

**Basic** - Simple model:

```python
from eden.db import Model
from eden.db.fields import f

class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    
    title: str = f(max_length=200)
    content: str = f()
    published: bool = f(default=False)
```

### How It Works

Eden models use SQLAlchemy 2.0's `Mapped` type annotations:

```python
field_name: python_type = FieldHelper(**options)
```

---

## 2.3 Field Types

### String Fields

```python
class User(Model):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    
    username: str = f(
        max_length=50,
        unique=True,
        index=True,
        nullable=False,
        default="",
    )
```

### Numeric Fields

```python
from eden.db.fields import f, FloatField

class Product(Model):
    __tablename__ = "products"
    
    price: float = FloatField(default=0.0)
    quantity: int = f(default=0)
```

### Boolean Field

```python
class User(Model):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    
    is_active: bool = f(default=True)
    is_admin: bool = f(default=False)
```

### DateTime Fields

```python
from datetime import datetime
from eden.db.fields import f

class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    
    created_at: datetime = f(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at: datetime = f(auto_now=True)
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `auto_now_add` | Set on creation only |
| `auto_now` | Update on every save |

### UUID Fields

```python
import uuid
from eden.db.fields import f

class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    
    id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = f(primary_key=True, default_factory=uuid.uuid4)
```

### Text Field

```python
from eden.db.fields import TextField

class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    
    content: str = TextField()
```

---

## 2.4 Primary Keys

**Basic** - Default auto-increment:

```python
class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    pass  # Creates auto-incrementing `id`
```

**Basic** - UUID primary key:

```python
import uuid
class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = f(primary_key=True, default_factory=uuid.uuid4)
```

---

## 2.5 Creating Records

**Basic** - Create and save:

```python
post = Post(title="My First Post", content="Hello, World!")
await post.save()

post = await Post.create(title="Hello", content="World")
```

---

## 2.6 Reading Records

**Basic** - Get by ID:

```python
post = await Post.get(post_id)
post = await Post.get_or_none(post_id)
post = await Post.get_or_404(post_id)
```

**Basic** - Query with filters:

```python
posts = await Post.query().filter(published=True).all()
posts = await Post.query().filter(status="draft", author_id=1).all()
```

---

## 2.7 Updating Records

**Basic** - Update single:

```python
post = await Post.get(post_id)
post.title = "Updated Title"
await post.save()
```

**Basic** - Bulk update:

```python
await Post.query().filter(published=False).update(published=True)
```

---

## 2.8 Deleting Records

**Basic** - Delete:

```python
post = await Post.get(post_id)
await post.delete()

await Post.query().filter(status="draft").delete()
```

---

## 2.9 Relationships

### One-to-Many

```python
class Comment(Model):
    __tablename__ = "comments"
    content: str = TextField()
    post_id: int = ForeignKeyField("posts.id", ondelete="CASCADE")
    post: "Post" = Relationship("Post", back_populates="comments")

class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    title: str = f()
    comments: list["Comment"] = Relationship("Comment", back_populates="post")
```

### Many-to-Many

```python
class Tag(Model):
    __tablename__ = "tags"
    name: str = f(unique=True)
    posts: list["Post"] = ManyToManyField("Post", through="post_tags")

class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    title: str = f()
    tags: list["Tag"] = ManyToManyField("Tag", through="post_tags")
```

---

## 2.10 Advanced Queries

### Q Objects

```python
from eden.db.lookups import Q

posts = await Post.query().filter(
    Q(published=True) | Q(featured=True)
).all()

posts = await Post.query().filter(~Q(status="draft")).all()
```

### Lookup Extensions

```python
posts = await Post.query().filter(title__icontains="python").all()
posts = await Post.query().filter(view_count__gte=100).all()
posts = await Post.query().filter(status__in=["draft", "published"]).all()
```

| Lookup | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `__exact` | Exact match |
| `__icontains` | Case-insensitive contains |
| `__gte` | Greater than or equal |
| `__lte` | Less than or equal |
| `__in` | In list |
| `__isnull` | Null check |
| `__range` | Between range |

---

## 2.11 Ordering and Pagination

**Basic** - Order:

```python
posts = await Post.query().order_by("-created_at").all()
posts = await Post.query().order_by("-created_at", "title").all()
```

**Basic** - Pagination:

```python
from eden.db import Page

page = await Post.query().paginate(page=1, per_page=10)
print(page.items)
print(page.total)
print(page.has_next)
```

---

## 2.12 Aggregations

```python
from eden.db.aggregates import Count, Sum, Avg

stats = await Post.query().aggregate(
    total=Count("id"),
    avg_views=Avg("view_count")
)

posts = await Post.query().annotate(comment_count=Count("comments")).all()
```

---

## 2.13 F Expressions (Atomic Updates)

```python
from eden.db.lookups import F

await Post.query().filter(id=post_id).update(view_count=F("view_count") + 1)
```

---

## 2.14 Complete Example

```python
# models.py
import uuid
from datetime import datetime
from eden.db import Model
from eden.db.fields import f, TextField, f, ForeignKeyField

class User(Model):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    username: str = f(max_length=50, unique=True, index=True)
    email: str = f(max_length=255, unique=True)
    bio: str = TextField(nullable=True)
    is_active: bool = f(default=True)
    created_at: datetime = f(auto_now_add=True)

class Post(Model):
    __tablename__ = "posts"
    title: str = f(max_length=200)
    slug: str = f(max_length=200, unique=True, index=True)
    content: str = TextField()
    published: bool = f(default=False)
    view_count: int = f(default=0)
    author_id: Mapped[uuid.UUID] = ForeignKeyField("users.id", ondelete="CASCADE")
    created_at: datetime = f(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at: datetime = f(auto_now=True)
```

---

## Summary

You learned:
- Database setup and configuration
- Creating models with fields
- CRUD operations
- Relationships (One-to-Many, Many-to-Many)
- Complex queries with Q objects
- Lookups and filters
- Pagination and ordering
- Aggregations
- Atomic updates with F expressions

---
